name: Quince-And-Cranberry Compote
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT EACH QUINCE INTO QUARTERS, then cut quarters into pieces about ½-inch thick.
2. Grasp each piece firmly and cut away the peel and core with a sharp knife.
3. When all slices have been cored and peeled, cut each piece into small chunks.
4. In a saucepan, combine water, sugar, spices, and orange peel.
5. Bring mixture to boil, stirring to dissolve sugar.
6. Add quince. Reduce heat, cover pan, and cook very slowly until fruit has turned a deep pink color, about 2 hours.
7. If cooked slowly, there should still be quite a bit of liquid in the pan when the fruit is done.
8. Sort through cranberries, remove those that are not in good shape, and rinse the rest.
9. Add cranberries to cooked quince.
10. If the mixture seems too dry, add a little more water or cranberry juice.
11. Increase heat slightly and cook cranberries until they begin to pop open, 12-to-15 minutes.
12. Use a rubber scraper to gently mix them with quince.
13. When done, refrigerate compote to cool.
14. Stir in enough vinegar to balance the sweetness.
